name: ack
description: Search for PATTERN in each source file in the tree from the current directory on down

name: -i
--ignore-case
options.description: Ignore case distinctions in PATTERN

name: -S
--smart-case
options.description: Ignore case distinctions in PATTERN, only if PATTERN contains no upper case. Ignored if -i or -I are specified

options.name: --nosmart-case
options.description: Disable smart-case

name: -I
--no-ignore-case
options.description: Turns on case-sensitivity in PATTERN. Negates -i and --smart-case

name: -v
--invert-match
options.description: Invert match: select non-matching lines

name: -w
--word-regexp
options.description: Force PATTERN to match only whole words

name: -Q
--literal
options.description: Quote all metacharacters; PATTERN is literal

options.name: --range-start
options.description: Specify PATTERN as the start of a match range

options.name: --range-end
options.description: Specify PATTERN as the end of a match range

options.name: --match
options.description: Specify PATTERN explicitly. Typically omitted

options.name: --not
options.description: Specifies PATTERN that must not be found on the line for a match to occur

options.name: --lines
options.description: Only print line(s) NUM of each file

name: -l
--files-with-matches
options.description: Print filenames with at least one match

name: -L
--files-without-matches
options.description: Print filenames with no matches

options.name: --output
options.description: Output the evaluation of Perl expression for each line (turns off text highlighting)

options.name: -o
options.description: Show only the part of a line matching PATTERN. Same as --output='$&'

options.name: --passthru
options.description: Print all lines, whether matching or not

name: -m
--max-count
options.description: Stop searching in each file after NUM matches
Stop searching after one match of any kind

name: -H
--with-filename
options.description: Print the filename for each match (default: on unless explicitly searching a single file)

name: -h
--no-filename
options.description: Suppress the prefixing filename on output

name: -c
--count
options.description: Print filenames and count of matching lines

options.name: --column
options.description: Show the column number of the first match

options.name: --nocolumn
options.description: Don't show the column number of the first match

name: -A
--after-context
options.description: Print NUM lines of trailing context after matching lines

name: -B
--before-context
options.description: Print NUM lines of leading context before matching lines

name: -C
--context
options.description: Print NUM lines (default 2) of output context

options.name: --print0
options.description: Print null byte as separator between filenames, only works with -f, -g, -l, -L or -c

options.name: -s
options.description: Suppress error messages about nonexistent or unreadable files

options.name: --pager
options.description: Pipes all ack output through COMMAND

options.name: --nopager
options.description: Do not send output through a pager

options.name: --heading
options.description: Print a filename heading above each file's results (default: on when used interactively)

options.name: --noheading
options.description: Don't print a filename heading above each file's results

options.name: --break
options.description: Print a break between results from different files (default: on when used interactively)

options.name: --nobreak
options.description: Don't print a break between results from different files

options.name: --group
options.description: Same as --heading --break

options.name: --nogroup
options.description: Same as --noheading --nobreak

name: -p
--proximate
options.description: Separate match output with blank lines unless they are within LINES lines from each other

options.name: --underline
options.description: Print a line of carets under the matched text

options.name: --nounderline
options.description: Don't print a line of carets under the matched text

options.name: --color
options.description: Highlight the matching text (default: on unless output is redirected)

options.name: --nocolor
options.description: Don't highlight the matching text

options.name: --colour
options.description: Highlight the matching text (default: on unless output is redirected)

options.name: --nocolour
options.description: Don't highlight the matching text

options.name: --color-filename
options.description: Set the color for filenames

options.name: --color-match
options.description: Set the color for matches

options.name: --color-colno
options.description: Set the color for column numbers

options.name: --color-lineno
options.description: Set the color for line numbers

options.name: --help-colors
options.description: Show a list of possible color combinations

options.name: --help-rgb-colors
options.description: Show a list of advanced RGB colors

options.name: --flush
options.description: Flush output immediately, even when ack is used non-interactively

options.name: -f
options.description: Only print the files selected, without searching. The PATTERN must not be specified

options.name: -g
options.description: Same as -f, but only select files matching PATTERN

options.name: --sort-files
options.description: Sort the found files lexically

options.name: --show-types
options.description: Show which types each file has

options.name: --files-from
options.description: Read the list of files to search from FILE

options.name: -x
options.description: Read the list of files to search from STDIN

options.name: --ignore-dir
options.description: Add directory to list of ignored dirs

options.name: --ignore-directory
options.description: Add directory to list of ignored dirs (synonym for --ignore-dir)

options.name: --noignore-dir
options.description: Remove directory from list of ignored dirs

options.name: --noignore-directory
options.description: Remove directory from list of ignored dirs (synonym for --noignore-dir)

options.name: --ignore-file
options.description: Add filter for ignoring files (format: FILTER:ARGS)

name: -r
-R
--recurse
options.description: Recurse into subdirectories (default: on)

name: -n
--no-recurse
options.description: No descending into subdirectories

options.name: --follow
options.description: Follow symlinks

options.name: --nofollow
options.description: Don't follow symlinks (default)

name: -t
--type
options.description: Include only X files, where X is a filetype (e.g. python, html, markdown)

options.name: -T
options.description: Exclude X files, where X is a filetype

name: -k
--known-types
options.description: Include only files of types that ack recognizes

options.name: --help-types
options.description: Display all known types, and how they're defined

options.name: --type-set
options.description: Replaces definition of type (format: TYPE:FILTER:ARGS)

options.name: --type-add
options.description: Add to definition of type (format: TYPE:FILTER:ARGS)

options.name: --type-del
options.description: Removes all filters associated with type

options.name: --noenv
options.description: Ignore environment variables and global ackrc files

options.name: --ackrc
options.description: Specify an ackrc file to use

options.name: --ignore-ack-defaults
options.description: Ignore default definitions included with ack

options.name: --create-ackrc
options.description: Outputs a default ackrc for your customization to standard output

options.name: --dump
options.description: Dump information on which options are loaded and where they're defined

options.name: --filter
options.description: Force ack to treat standard input as a pipe

options.name: --nofilter
options.description: Force ack to treat standard input as a tty

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help

options.name: --man
options.description: Print the manual

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version and copyright

options.name: --thpppt
options.description: Bill the Cat

options.name: --bar
options.description: The warning admiral

options.name: --cathy
options.description: Chocolate! Chocolate! Chocolate!
